speaker: MACBETH
text: Bring me no more reports. Let them fly all.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Till Birnam Wood remove to Dunsinane

speaker: MACBETH
text: I cannot taint with fear. What’s the boy Malcolm?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Was he not born of woman? The spirits that know

speaker: MACBETH
text: All mortal consequences have pronounced me thus:

speaker: MACBETH
text: “Fear not, Macbeth. No man that’s born of woman

speaker: MACBETH
text: Shall e’er have power upon thee.” Then fly, false thanes,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And mingle with the English epicures.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The mind I sway by and the heart I bear

speaker: MACBETH
text: Shall never sag with doubt nor shake with fear.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The devil damn thee black, thou cream-faced loon!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Where got’st thou that goose-look?

speaker: SERVANT
text: There is ten thousand—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Geese, villain?

speaker: SERVANT
text: Soldiers, sir.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Go prick thy face and over-red thy fear,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou lily-livered boy. What soldiers, patch?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Death of thy soul! Those linen cheeks of thine

speaker: MACBETH
text: Are counselors to fear. What soldiers, whey-face?

speaker: SERVANT
text: The English force, so please you.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Take thy face hence.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Seyton!—I am sick at heart

speaker: MACBETH
text: When I behold—Seyton, I say!—This push

speaker: MACBETH
text: Will cheer me ever or disseat me now.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I have lived long enough. My way of life

speaker: MACBETH
text: Is fall’n into the sere, the yellow leaf,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And that which should accompany old age,

speaker: MACBETH
text: As honor, love, obedience, troops of friends,

speaker: MACBETH
text: I must not look to have, but in their stead

speaker: MACBETH
text: Curses, not loud but deep, mouth-honor, breath

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which the poor heart would fain deny and dare not.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Seyton!

speaker: SEYTON
text: What’s your gracious pleasure?

speaker: MACBETH
text: What news more?

speaker: SEYTON
text: All is confirmed, my lord, which was reported.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I’ll fight till from my bones my flesh be hacked.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Give me my armor.

speaker: SEYTON
text: ’Tis not needed yet.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I’ll put it on.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Send out more horses. Skirr the country round.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Hang those that talk of fear. Give me mine armor.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: How does your patient, doctor?

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Not so sick, my lord,

speaker: DOCTOR
text: As she is troubled with thick-coming fancies

speaker: DOCTOR
text: That keep her from her rest.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Cure her of that.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Canst thou not minister to a mind diseased,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Pluck from the memory a rooted sorrow,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Raze out the written troubles of the brain,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And with some sweet oblivious antidote

speaker: MACBETH
text: Cleanse the stuffed bosom of that perilous stuff

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which weighs upon the heart?

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Therein the patient

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Must minister to himself.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Throw physic to the dogs. I’ll none of it.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Come, put mine armor on. Give me my staff.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Seyton, send out.—Doctor, the thanes fly from me.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Come, sir, dispatch.—If thou couldst, doctor, cast

speaker: MACBETH
text: The water of my land, find her disease,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And purge it to a sound and pristine health,

speaker: MACBETH
text: I would applaud thee to the very echo

speaker: MACBETH
text: That should applaud again.—Pull ’t off, I say.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: What rhubarb, senna, or what purgative drug

speaker: MACBETH
text: Would scour these English hence? Hear’st thou of them?

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Ay, my good lord. Your royal preparation

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Makes us hear something.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Bring it after me.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: I will not be afraid of death and bane

speaker: MACBETH
text: Till Birnam Forest come to Dunsinane.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Were I from Dunsinane away and clear,

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Profit again should hardly draw me here.
